one_time_description: Unrealized gains on remeasurement of BMO Private Banking investment portfolio
economic_notes: Nation owns several enterprises including Colchester Holdings Ltd., Akamihk Kanataskiy Ventures Ltd. (AKV), Akamihk Energy Incorporated (AEI), MFN Management Inc., Killarney Lake Solar LP, Akamihk Health Care Services Inc., and Akamihk Child and Family Services Society. AKV had revenues of $12,538,551 and AEI had revenues of $3,506,800. Nation has ongoing TLE (Treaty Land Entitlement) and Cows & Plows negotiations with bank loans funding legal fees. Killarney Lake Solar LP has assets of $18,435,545.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to Nation not identifying Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) liabilities under PS 3280 for buildings potentially containing asbestos. Seven entities accounted for by equity method (GBEs). Bank loan of $3,749,001 from RBC for TLE legal fees is included in accounts receivable as it will be repaid by Federal Government upon successful settlement or by insurance if unsuccessful. 2023 comparative figures restated to include ATB loan of $2,576,636 for TLE legal costs. OSR cannot be determined as revenue lines include deferred revenue netting which makes government vs own-source split unclear; Schedule 3 shows Own Source Revenue program with $609,342 surplus. No schedule of remuneration included in financial statements. Accounts payable increased dramatically from $174,104 to $2,853,125. Excess of revenue over expenditures on Statement of Financial Activities ($1,499,369) differs from Change in Net Financial Assets statement ($1,211,617) due to subsidiary equity earnings inclusion.
